3.happening: Create the empty list of answer indices.
3.why: We collect every window start that turns out to be an anagram.
3.invariant: res holds all matches found so far.

4.happening: p ({len(p)} chars) is longer than s ({len(s)} chars) — no anagram can fit.
4.why: A window of length |p| doesn't even fit inside s.
4.invariant: An anagram of p is exactly |p| characters long.

4.happening: p fits inside s, so anagrams are possible.
4.why: The window of length |p| can slide across s.
4.invariant: An anagram of p is exactly |p| characters long.

5.happening: Return the empty list — there is nothing to find.
5.why: No window of length |p| exists in s.

6.why: An anagram is defined purely by letter counts, so we compare counts, not order.
6.invariant: need will hold the exact letter counts of p.

7.why: This tracks the letters currently inside the sliding window.
7.invariant: window holds the letter counts of the current window.

8.why: We fill both maps over the first |p| characters.

9.why: Both maps must be fully built before the first comparison.
9.invariant: Characters before {i} are already counted in both maps.

9.happening: Both maps are primed over the first window.
9.why: Now the first window can be compared to the target.
9.invariant: need and window each cover exactly {len(p)} characters.

10.why: It contributes to the target count.
10.invariant: pc is a single letter of p.

11.happening: '{pc}' is already in the target map — increment it.
11.why: A repeated pattern letter raises its required count.
11.invariant: Each distinct letter has one entry.

11.happening: '{pc}' is new to the target map.
11.invariant: Each distinct letter has one entry.

12.happening: Raise the target count of '{pc}' to {need[pc] + 1}.
12.why: The pattern needs this many of that letter.
12.invariant: need[pc] equals how many '{pc}' appear in p.

14.happening: Create the target entry for '{pc}' with count 1.
14.why: First time this pattern letter is seen.
14.invariant: need[pc] equals how many '{pc}' appear in p.

15.happening: Take window character '{s[i]}' from the first window.
15.why: It contributes to the window count.
15.invariant: sc is a single letter of the first window.

16.happening: '{sc}' is already in the window map — increment it.
16.why: A repeated window letter raises its count.
16.invariant: Each distinct letter has one entry.

16.happening: '{sc}' is new to the window map.
16.invariant: Each distinct letter has one entry.

17.happening: Raise the window count of '{sc}' to {window[sc] + 1}.
17.why: The window now holds another copy of that letter.
17.invariant: window[sc] equals how many '{sc}' are in the current window.

19.happening: Create the window entry for '{sc}' with count 1.
19.why: First time this window letter is seen.
19.invariant: window[sc] equals how many '{sc}' are in the current window.

20.why: Count the next character of the first window.
20.invariant: Each of the first |p| characters is counted once.

21.happening: Set the window's left edge to index 0.
21.why: The first window covers indices 0 through |p|-1.
21.invariant: The window always spans exactly |p| characters.

22.happening: Set the window's right edge to index {len(p) - 1}.
22.why: That completes the first window of length |p|.

23.happening: The window map already equals the target — the first window is an anagram!
23.why: Equal counts mean the window is a rearrangement of p.
23.invariant: A match means the window is an anagram of p.

23.happening: The first window's counts differ from the target.
23.why: At least one letter's count doesn't match, so it isn't an anagram.
23.invariant: Only exact count-equality counts as a match.

24.happening: The first window matched — record it.
24.invariant: res only ever holds true anagram starts.

25.happening: Append start index {left} to the answer.
25.why: This window is an anagram, so its start belongs in the result.
25.invariant: res grows by one valid index.

26.happening: The right edge ({right}) can still move — slide the window.
26.why: Every window position of length |p| must be checked.
26.invariant: The window slides one step per iteration.

26.happening: The right edge reached the end of s — every window has been checked.
26.why: There are no more positions to test.
26.invariant: All anagram starts have been found.

27.happening: Reset the match flag for the new position.
27.why: The previous window's result must not carry over.
27.invariant: matched reflects only the current window.

28.happening: Extend the right edge to index {right + 1}.
28.why: A new letter is about to enter the window on the right.
28.invariant: The window is momentarily |p|+1 wide until the left edge catches up.

29.why: Sliding right adds this letter to the window.
29.invariant: enter is the letter joining the window.

30.happening: '{enter}' is already in the window — increment it.
30.why: The window now holds one more of that letter.
First time this letter appears in the window — create its entry.

31.happening: Raise the window count of '{enter}' to {window[enter] + 1}.
31.why: One more copy of that letter is inside the window.
31.invariant: window[enter] equals its count in the window.

33.happening: Create the window entry for '{enter}' with count 1.
33.why: This letter just entered the window.
33.invariant: window[enter] equals its count in the window.

34.why: Sliding right drops the leftmost letter out of the window.
34.invariant: leave is the letter leaving the window.

35.happening: Lower the window count of '{leave}' to {window[leave] - 1}.
35.why: One fewer copy of that letter is inside the window now.
35.invariant: window[leave] equals its remaining count in the window.

36.happening: '{leave}' has count 0 now — it must be removed.
36.why: A leftover zero entry would stop the window map ever matching the target exactly.
36.invariant: The window map holds only letters actually present.

36.happening: '{leave}' still has copies left in the window.
36.why: It stays in the map with its reduced count.
36.invariant: The window map holds only letters actually present.

37.happening: Remove '{leave}' from the window map entirely.
37.why: Dropping zero-count keys is what lets the two maps ever be exactly equal.
37.invariant: No zero-count letters linger in the map.

38.happening: Advance the left edge to index {left + 1}.
38.why: The window is back to length |p| after the slide.

39.happening: The window map equals the target — this window is an anagram!
39.why: Equal counts mean the window is a rearrangement of p.
39.invariant: A match means the window is an anagram of p.

39.happening: The window map differs from the target.
39.why: Some letter's count is off, so this window is not an anagram.
39.invariant: Only exact count-equality counts as a match.

40.happening: This window matched — record its start.
40.invariant: res only ever holds true anagram starts.

41.happening: Append start index {left} to the answer.
41.why: This window is an anagram, so its start belongs in the result.
41.invariant: res grows by one valid index.

42.why: Every window of length |p| has been checked exactly once.

init.happening: Setting up the result list and the two empty count maps.
init.why: We compare letter counts, so we need a map for the target and one for the window.

loop.happening: Priming the first window, or sliding the window across s.
loop.why: One pass builds the counts, another slides and compares.
loop.invariant: The window always spans exactly |p| characters once sliding.

check.happening: Testing a letter's presence or comparing the window map to the target.
check.why: Equality of the two maps is exactly the anagram condition.
check.invariant: No counts change on a pure check line.

update.happening: Adjusting a letter count, dropping a zero entry, or recording a match.
update.why: The window map and the result list are the state that matters.
update.invariant: The window map holds only letters actually present.

move.happening: Advancing the left or right edge of the window.
move.why: The fixed-size window slides one step at a time.

return.why: All window positions have been checked.
